data.title: Sweden orders $916M in air defense, counter-drone systems
data.description:
Sweden plans to acquire air defense and counter-drone capabilities valued at about 8.7 billion Swedish crowns, or roughly $916 million, as part of a broader effort to strengthen national security, Defense Minister Pal Jonson said Thursday.

The procurement will involve multiple suppliers, including Saab (SAABF) (SAABY) and BAE Systems (BAESF) (BAESY). It comes as Sweden, alongside other European nations, continues to increase defense spending following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Military expenditures are projected to reach 2.8% of gross domestic product by 2026, with further increases expected later in the decade.

According to Jonson, the new systems are intended to expand Sweden’s current air defense coverage, which has primarily focused on protecting military forces. The additional capabilities are expected to improve protection for civilian areas and key infrastructure, including transportation hubs and energy facilities.

The package includes the Gute II system, a counter-drone platform that combines radar detection with cannon-based interception. The system can be deployed either on the ground or mounted on vehicles. Jonson said similar technologies have been used in Ukraine and have demonstrated effectiveness against drone threats such as the Iranian-designed Shahed drone.

In a separate statement, Saab said its portion of the contract is valued at approximately 2.6 billion crowns and will focus on systems designed to identify and neutralize low-flying drones.

The broader procurement also covers ammunition and military vehicles, including infantry mobility platforms produced by Finnish manufacturer Sisu Auto.

Deliveries of the systems are expected to take place between 2027 and 2028.

data.title: Microsoft releases trio of AI models for transcription, voice generation and image creation
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) released a trio of new artificial intelligence models built in-house, which are available at competitive pricing, pushing them into direct competition with offerings from Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), OpenAI (OPENAI), and a range of AI startups. 

"We're bringing our growing MAI model family to every developer in Foundry, including MAI-Transcribe-1, the most accurate transcription model in the world across 25 languages; MAI-Voice-1, natural, expressive speech generation; and MAI-Image-2, our most capable image model yet," said Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella in a post on X. 

Users can access these new models through Microsoft Foundry and the MAI Playground. Prices for MAI-Transcribe-1 start at $0.36 per hour, MAI-Voice-1 starts at $22 per 1M characters, and MAI-Image-2 starts at $5 per 1M tokens for text input and $33 per 1M tokens for image output.

In comparison, Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image generation model runs $120 per 1M tokens, and Gemini 3.1 Flash Image costs $60 per 1M tokens.

Microsoft said its MAI-Transcribe-1 is the most accurate transcription model on the market. Across all languages, it produced a mean word error rate of 3.9%. OpenAI's GPT-Transcribe had a 4.2% error rate, Gemini 3.1 Flash had a 4.9% error rate, and OpenAI's Whisper-large-v3 had a 7.6% error rate.

"At Microsoft AI, we're building Humanist AI," said Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of Microsoft AI. "We have a distinct view when creating our AI models—putting humans at the center, optimizing for how people actually communicate, training for practical use. You'll see more models from us soon in Foundry and directly in Microsoft products and experiences."

data.title: EPR Properties tops mid-cap ROE growth list, as market prepares to ride out volatility
data.description:
Higher rates are a growing headwind for stocks: the higher they go, the more pressure they put on equities. After a month of conflict, strategists are likely to start lowering year-end targets as oil prices keep inflation fears alive and the market stays focused on just one big issue.

For now, the best approach is to ride out the volatility by owning stocks with strong company-specific strength, experts suggest. From a quant view, that means names with high return on equity and solid EPS growth.

Below is a list of the top 10 mid cap stocks ranked by their year over year ROE growth. The list includes EPS revisions for each company.

The list is topped by EPR Properties (EPR), with a year over year ROE growth of over 93%. Credit Acceptance Corporation (CACC) and PHINIA Inc. (PHIN) are next, with Phillips Edison & Company, Inc. (PECO) and Trinity Industries, Inc. (TRN) rounding out the rest of the top five.

The list features diverse sectors including Construction Partners, Inc. (ROAD) in construction and engineering, Kaiser Aluminum Corporation (KALU) in aluminum, and Sotera Health Company (SHC) in life sciences tools and services. Archrock, Inc. (AROC) and Enova International, Inc. (ENVA) complete the top ten.

Here is the list:

  1. EPR Properties (EPR), YOY ROE growth: 93.27%, EPS revision: A

  2. Credit Acceptance Corporation (CACC), YOY ROE growth: 83.02%, EPS revision: A-

  3. PHINIA Inc. (PHIN), YOY ROE growth: 80.18%, EPS revision: A+

  4. Phillips Edison & Company, Inc. (PECO), YOY ROE growth: 78.60%, EPS revision: A-

  5. Trinity Industries, Inc. (TRN), YOY ROE growth: 74.80%, EPS revision: A

  6. Construction Partners, Inc. (ROAD), YOY ROE growth: 62.96%, EPS revision: A+

  7. Kaiser Aluminum Corporation (KALU), YOY ROE growth: 52.26%, EPS revision: A

  8. Sotera Health Company (SHC), YOY ROE growth: 47.38%, EPS revision: A

  9. Archrock, Inc. (AROC), YOY ROE growth: 45.88%, EPS revision: A-

  10. Enova International, Inc. (ENVA), YOY ROE growth: 41.63%, EPS revision: A+

Mid-Cap ETFs: (IJH), (VO), (IWR), (MDY), (VOE), (VOT), (SCHM), (IJK), and (IJJ)

data.title: Iran and Oman are drafting a protocol to monitor Strait of Hormuz traffic – report
data.description:
Iran is drafting a protocol with Oman to monitor traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, according to the state-run Islamic Republic News Agency, Bloomberg reported.

Iran Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi has reportedly said that vessel traffic through the strait should take place under Iran and Oman’s supervision and coordination.

“Naturally, these requirements do not constitute restrictions but are intended to facilitate and ensure safe passage and provide better services to ships transiting this route,” the deputy foreign minister told IRNA.

data.title: Wall Street pares losses as investors digest Trump’s address to the nation
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages turned momentarily positive on Thursday as markets assessed President Donald Trump’s address to the nation, signaling a potential escalation in military action against Iran.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last at -0.2%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, five of the 11 S&P segments traded in the red, with consumer discretionary at the bottom. At the other end of the spectrum, real estate was the strongest performer so far. 

Trump said the U.S. will strike Iran “extremely hard” over the next two to three weeks, warning the country would be pushed “back to the stone ages,” though he indicated talks with Tehran remain ongoing. 

Treasury yields were mostly muted across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 2 basis points lower at 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) dropped 2 basis points to 3.80%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) lost 2 basis points to 4.88%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims  for the week ended March 28 decreased by 9K to 202K, compared to the prior week's revised level of 211K and against the 213K consensus figure.

As for stocks that were on the move, APA Corporation (APA) climbed +1.1%, while shares of United Airlines (UAL) dropped -3.9%.

data.title: Reducing banks' need for reserves is better approach to shrink Fed's balance sheet: Dallas Fed's Lorie Logan
data.description:
Shifting the demand curve inward by reducing banks' need for reserves is a better approach to shrink the Fed's balance sheet than running up the demand curve to scarce reserves, according to Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan.

Lorie Logan was speaking at a fireside chat before the Eleventh District Banking Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

"The growth of the Fed's balance sheet has prompted a lot of discussion," the FOMC's voting member noted.

"Balance sheet growth isn't bad if it serves the public," she said. "But neither should we waste balance sheet space and let it become a distraction from our mission."

"We can always offer more liquidity than the economy demands, but if we don't meet the demand, financial pressures will arise," according to Logan.

Before 2000, the Fed used the different system of scarce reserves, in which reserves earned no interest. The spread made it costly for banks to hold reserves, and then holding the reserves was tried to economize, and hence the name scarce, she said.

"Policymakers could take steps to reduce banks' need for reserves. Then a smaller quantity of reserves would meet banks' demand with market rates near interest on reserve balances. Our economists would call that shifting the demand curve inward or shifting the demand curve to the left," said Logan.

If we reduce banks' need for reserves so that the demand curve shifts inward or to the left, we are still meeting banks' demands. But if we run up the demand curve, and return to scarce reserves, we're pushing up market rates relative to interest on reserve balances, and we're putting a price on reserves that banks don't face today, according to the Dallas Fed president.

However, she said that the precise method of shifting the demand curve inward matters.

"Reserve holdings that make the banking system safer represent an efficient and effective use of our balance sheet, and I wouldn't want to discourage that," said Logan.

"On the other hand, some liquidity regulations can push banks to hold reserve bumpers. But then discourage banks from putting those buffers to use in a crisis. Regulations like that might boost reserve holdings without necessarily making the financial system safer," she noted.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: US DOJ to appeal court order pausing ban on Anthropic's AI
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice filed a notice in court on Thursday to appeal a federal judge’s order blocking a ban on government use of Anthropic's (ANTHRO) AI technology.

The DOJ filed the notice in the case of U.S. Department of War versus Anthropic in the U.S. District Court, Northern District of California (San Francisco).

Anthropic and DOJ did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Last week, U.S. District Judge Rita F. Lin issued a preliminary injunction, halting the Trump administration’s plan to cut ties with Anthropic while the case proceeds in San Francisco federal court. The order was stayed for seven days to allow the government time to appeal.

Anthropic sued the U.S. Department of War after the department dropped its contract with the AI startup and labeled it a supply chain risk. Anthropic said in court filings that the U.S. government’s action of blacklisting the company could reduce the company's 2026 revenue by multiple billions of dollars.

The Claude AI chatbot maker — which is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google — had rejected the Department of War's demand for unrestricted access to its AI models.

data.title: Pfizer RSV vaccine offered to 3M more older adults in England
data.description:
England’s National Health Service announced on Thursday that three million more older adults in the country will be eligible to receive Pfizer’s (PFE) respiratory syncytial virus ((RSV)) vaccine, Abrysvo.

Accordingly, people aged 80 years and older, as well as those living in a care home for older adults, will be able to receive Abrysvo via a GP practice, community pharmacies, or by informing employees in care homes.

The decision, taking effect on Apr. 2, is expected to prevent potentially thousands of hospital admissions caused by the virus, which causes bronchitis and pneumonia, among other symptoms, NHS England said. The RSV accounts for nearly 9,000 hospitalizations in people older than 75 in the U.K. each year.

Previously, the NHS offered the bivalent shot as part of its RSV vaccination programs for adults aged 75-79 years old since the program launched in Sept. 2024. Pregnant women also received Abrysvo from 28 weeks of their pregnancy to protect newborns from RSV.

data.title: Oil soars to $110/bbl as Trump threatens war escalation; Macron says unrealistic to take Hormuz
data.description:
U.S. crude oil futures skyrocketed past $110/bbl Thursday after President ‌Trump rattled markets with his promise to escalate attacks on Iran over the next 2-3 weeks, sparking fears of prolonged disruptions to oil supply.

Meanwhile, French President Macron ‌said it would be "unrealistic" to launch a military operation to force open the Strait of Hormuz, after Trump challenged countries most impacted by the shutoff to work towards reopening it.

U.S. front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery surged 11.3% to $111.49/bbl, the highest in four years, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery soared 7% to $108.28/bbl, still below highs near $120 touched earlier in the conflict.

Oil and gas equities, which fell sharply in the previous session, turned broadly higher in Thursday's trading - although below highs posted at the open - led by Diamondback Energy (FANG) up 3%, Texas Pacific Land (TPL) up 2%, ConocoPhillips (COP) up 1.9%, Chevron (CVX) up 1.8%.

Markets are reacting to the absence of any "clear mention of ceasefire or diplomatic engagement" in the speech, Phillip Nova senior analyst Priyanka Sachdeva said in a note. "If tensions intensify or maritime risks increase, oil could test fresh ​highs as markets price in potential supply disruptions."

OPEC+ reportedly will consider a further oil output increase when eight members meet on ‌Sunday, a move that would position key producers to add more barrels if the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)

data.title: U.S. Navy destroyer U.S.S. George M. Neal launched at Ingalls Shipbuilding
data.description:
The Ingalls Shipbuilding division of HII (HII) on Wednesday launched the future U.S.S. George M. Neal (DDG 131), marking a key milestone in construction of the U.S. Navy’s next generation of guided missile destroyers.

 

 

The vessel is the fourth Flight III Arleigh Burke-class destroyer to be built at the Mississippi shipyard. Prior to launch, shipbuilders completed structural work, installed major components and conducted inspections to prepare the ship for entry into the water.

The launch process involved moving the ship from land into a dry dock, where final checks were carried out before the dock was flooded, allowing the vessel to float for the first time.

The destroyer is named after George M. Neal, a Korean War aviation machinist’s mate who received the Navy Cross for attempting to rescue a fellow service member under enemy fire.

DDG 131 is part of the Flight III configuration, which includes updated systems intended to address evolving threats. These ships are equipped with the AN/SPY-6 ((V))1 radar and the Aegis Baseline 10 combat system, both designed to enhance air and missile defense capabilities.

Following the launch, the ship will undergo outfitting, activation of onboard systems and testing before entering sea trials, a standard phase ahead of delivery to the U.S. Navy.
